Abstract: A current limitation of Generative AI (GenAI) is its propensity to hallucinate. While Large Language Models (LLM) have taken the world by storm, without eliminating or at least reducing hallucination, real-world GenAI systems will likely continue to face challenges in user adoption. In the process of deploying an enterprise application that produces workflows from natural language requirements, we devised a system leveraging Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to improve the quality of the structured output that represents such workflows. Thanks to our implementation of RAG, our proposed system significantly reduces hallucination and allows the generalization of our LLM to out-of-domain settings. In addition, we show that using a small, well-trained retriever can reduce the size of the accompanying LLM at no loss in performance, thereby making deployments of LLM-based systems less resource-intensive. results.title: ServiceNow: Retain Data Control Amid AI Adoption Risks — Silicon Report
highlights:
ServiceNow: Retain Data Control Amid AI Adoption Risks — Silicon Report
...
ServiceNow is actively advising enterprises to prioritize data control as they integrate artificial intelligence solutions, cautioning against the risks of surrendering proprietary information for perceived intelligence gains. This position, highlighted by SiliconANGLE, highlights a growing tension between the rapid adoption of AI capabilities and the imperative for robust data governance within large organizations. The company's stance suggests that while AI promises significant operational efficiencies and insights, these benefits should not come at the expense of an enterprise's long-term control over its foundational data assets.
...
Siliconangle reported that the message from ServiceNow resonates with enterprise VPs managing complex AI deployments, where the temptation to leverage external, pre-trained models often involves sharing or transferring data to third-party providers. Such arrangements can introduce vulnerabilities related to data leakage, intellectual property exposure, and compliance challenges, particularly in regulated industries. For organizations reorganizing themselves to put AI at the center, the mechanism of data control becomes a critical architectural decision, influencing everything from model training strategies to data residency requirements. ServiceNow’s advocacy implies a focus on solutions that allow customers to retain data within their own secure environments, whether on-premises or in private cloud instances.
...
Siliconangle said this operational framing positions ServiceNow as a partner for secure AI integration, emphasizing the tradeoff between immediate intelligence gains from broad data pooling and the strategic necessity of maintaining a competitive advantage through proprietary data. Enterprise buyers must weigh the short-term allure of off-the-shelf AI against the potential long-term costs of diminished data sovereignty. The company's messaging suggests that its platform and AI offerings are designed to enable intelligence extraction without necessitating the relinquishment of data control, thereby addressing a core concern for organizations focused on security, compliance, and competitive differentiation in their AI strategies.
